response.justification: This candidate is a seasoned Staff/Senior Software Engineer with over 15 years of professional experience — the exact opposite of the entry-level profile this role targets. While Java appears in their skill list and was used briefly at Altom Consulting, their primary stack is TypeScript and Node.js. More critically, the role mandates strong German communication skills, and the candidate lists German as only 'Basic', which is a hard disqualifier for client-facing work in the German insurance sector.
strengths: Holds a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, satisfying the educational requirement
Demonstrated agile practice (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple roles
Java is listed as a skill with documented usage (robotic arms and mobile tooling at Altom)
concerns: Massively overqualified: 15+ years at senior and staff level — fundamentally incompatible with an entry-level position
German proficiency is 'Basic', directly failing the explicit requirement for strong written and verbal German
Java experience is incidental and dated; the candidate's actual expertise is TypeScript/Node.js, not Java-based architectures
